% The Seven Ecumenical Councils of the Undivided Church, edited by
% Henry R. Percival, text from CCEL's ThML edition of NPNF Second
% Series, Vol. XIV (councils-thml.xml, raw extract preserved). The
% whole volume except its title pages and indexes: Percival's preface
% and introductions, the seven councils with their canons, decrees,
% excursuses and notes, and the received local synods and appendix.
% The text lives in councils-body.tex, generated by ccel2tex.py; this
% file owns the preamble and front matter.
\documentclass[11pt,letterpaper]{article}
\ifdefined\pdfsuppressptexinfo\pdfsuppressptexinfo=-1 \fi
\ifdefined\pdftrailerid\pdftrailerid{}\fi
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
% Greek: with texlive-langgreek installed (LGR encoding + textalpha)
% Greek renders in Greek type; without it the build still succeeds.
\IfFileExists{textalpha.sty}{%
  \usepackage[LGR,T1]{fontenc}%
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}%
  \usepackage{textalpha}%
}{%
  \usepackage[T1]{fontenc}%
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}%
}
\ifdefined\ensuregreek
  \newcommand{\textgreek}[1]{\ensuregreek{#1}}
\else
  \newcommand{\textgreek}[1]{\emph{[Greek]}}
\fi
% pdflatex has no Hebrew font path here; the HTML edition keeps the
% real Hebrew, the PDF marks the spot.
\newcommand{\texthebrew}[1]{{\emph{[Hebrew]}}}
\usepackage{mathpazo}
\usepackage{microtype}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{needspace}
\usepackage[colorlinks=true,linkcolor=ink,urlcolor=maroon,citecolor=ink,filecolor=ink]{hyperref}
\urlstyle{same}
\hypersetup{pdftitle={The Seven Ecumenical Councils},pdfauthor={Henry R. Percival, ed.}}
\linespread{1.06}
\setlength{\emergencystretch}{1.5em}

\definecolor{ink}{HTML}{2A2521}
\definecolor{heading}{HTML}{8C4A32}
\definecolor{subhead}{HTML}{6E5642}
\definecolor{accent}{HTML}{B0894C}
\definecolor{maroon}{HTML}{7B2E2E}

% Divisions: new page, TOC section level.
\newcommand{\xchapter}[1]{%
  \clearpage\phantomsection\addcontentsline{toc}{section}{#1}%
  {\normalfont\fontsize{14}{17}\selectfont\bfseries\color{heading}%
   \raggedright\emergencystretch=1.5em #1\par}%
  \vspace{2pt}{\color{accent}\hrule height 1pt}\medskip}

% Documents within a division: TOC subsection level.
\newcommand{\xsection}[1]{%
  \par\vspace{1.2em}\phantomsection\addcontentsline{toc}{subsection}{#1}%
  \needspace{4\baselineskip}%
  {\normalfont\fontsize{12.5}{15.5}\selectfont\bfseries\color{heading}%
   \raggedright\emergencystretch=1.5em #1\par}%
  \vspace{2pt}{\color{accent}\hrule height 0.6pt}\smallskip}

% Canons and notes within a document, not in the TOC.
\newcommand{\xsubsection}[1]{%
  \needspace{3\baselineskip}\par\medskip
  {\normalfont\bfseries\color{subhead}\raggedright\emergencystretch=1.5em #1\par}%
  \nobreak\smallskip}

\begin{document}
\color{ink}

\begin{titlepage}
\centering
\vspace*{4em}
{\LARGE\bfseries\color{heading} The Seven Ecumenical Councils\par}
\vspace{1em}
{\large of the Undivided Church\par}
\vspace{2em}
{\itshape\color{subhead} Their canons and dogmatic decrees, together with the
canons of all the local synods which have received ecumenical
acceptance.\par}
\vspace{3em}
{Edited, with notes, by Henry R. Percival, M.A., D.D.\par}
\vfill
{\small From \emph{A Select Library of Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers of the
Christian Church}, Second Series, Volume XIV, edited by Philip Schaff and
Henry Wace, 1900. The translation is in the public domain.\par}
\vspace{2em}
\end{titlepage}

\tableofcontents

\input{councils-body.tex}

\end{document}
